headings: h3: Mark Pritchard Member of Parliament for The Wrekin
text_excerpt: About Mark | Mark Pritchard Skip to main content Mark Pritchard MP The Wrekin Main navigation Home About Mark News About The Wrekin Campaigns In Parliament Contact Mark Pritchard MP The Wrekin About Mark Tweet Mark has lived in Shropshire for the last 23 years. He is married to Tamuna and has one daughter and one step-daughter. He has been the Member of Parliament for The Wrekin since 2005. After leaving school, Mark studied theology. He then gained two Master's degrees in International Diplomacy, Marketing & Management, and a post-graduate Diploma in Marketing (Chartered Institute of Marketing) and a Diploma in Public Relations from the (Institute of Public Relations). Before entering Parliament in 2005, Mark ran a successful marketing business - advising a range of blue-chip clients and international businesses. He continues to run his own business, which he says "keeps me rooted in the real world - not just in the Westminster bubble". Mark's hobbies include tennis, writing on economic and foreign policy issues, and comedy. A keen footballer, Mark played one match for Hereford United Reserves - and still plays for the Parliamentary football team. Political Biography 2023 – present: Appointed OSCE’s Special Representative to the Arctic and High North (alongside role as OSCE PA Vice President) 2022 – 2025: Vice President of the Organisation for Security and Cooperation in Europe (OSCE PA). Elected by 57 nations in this security alliance 2021: Appointed by Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth II to the Privy Council. *This appointment continues under King Charles III* 2020 - 2022: Member of the UK’s Intelligence and Security Committee - overseeing the work of MI6, MI5, Defence Intelligence et al 2019 - 2022: Chairman of the UK Parliament’s Ukraine Caucus 2018 - 2020: Organisation for Security and Cooperation in Europe’s Special Envoy to Southeast Europe 2017 - 2020: Leader of UK delegation to Organisation for Security and Cooperation in Europe (OSCE PA) 2016 - 2018: Prime Minister's Trade & Investment Envoy to Armenia and Georgia 2012 - 2015: Member of the UK's delegation to the Council of Europe (PACE) 2012 – present (13 years): Speaker's Panel of the House of Commons. Chairing and overseeing Parliamentary debates and bills (e.g. chaired Modern Slavery Bill, Ivory Bill, Australia & New Zealand Trade Bill) 2010 - 2012: Secretary of the 1922 Committee (under Prime Minister Cameron) 2005 - 2011: Served on 8 Select Committees. Joint National Security Strategy Committee Joint-Human Rights International Development 2005 - 2010: UK representative to NATO Parliamentary Assembly 2005: Elected MP for The Wrekin. Longest serving MP in its history. *Re-elected in 2024 for a sixth time* One of only 6 Conservative MPs remaining from 2005 election.
